chief_complaint: A 57-year-old male presented with severe chest pain lasting several hours, accompanied by hemodynamic instability and ST-segment elevation consistent with myocardial infarction.
history: The patient presented with severe chest pain lasting several hours, accompanied by hemodynamic instability and ST-segment elevation consistent with myocardial infarction. He had no significant past medical history.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia, hypotension. Physical examination: diffuse chest pain, no focal abnormalities.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation. Coronary angiography (CAG): total occlusion of the left circumflex artery (LCX). Computed tomography angiography (CTA): absence of the right coronary artery (RCA).
correct_diagnosis: Misinterpretation of a left-dominant system with anomalous small RCA
differentials: Misinterpretation of a left-dominant system with anomalous small RCA
Total occlusion of the left circumflex artery (LCX)
Myocardial infarction due to coronary artery anomaly
correct_ranking: Misinterpretation of a left-dominant system with anomalous small RCA
Total occlusion of the left circumflex artery (LCX)
Myocardial infarction due to coronary artery anomaly
